TOP OF THE TIMES


TODAY'S HEADLINES | MONDAY, OCTOBER 15, 2007


Top Stories [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/1K25/PJ/h]
California/Local [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/TABC/PJ/h]
National [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/2DAX/PJ/h]
World [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/Y405/PJ/h]
Business [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/ZWCW/PJ/h]
Sports [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/RVT2/PJ/h]
Arts and Entertainment
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/V785/PJ/h]
Features [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/9JXN/PJ/h]

TOP STORIES [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/RVTM/PJ/h]


Follow the bouncing checks
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/KBFC/PJ/h]
By Kim Christensen
A reporter is victimized by a no-account forger, and the spending spree is on.
But a police report and solid documentation help limit the damage.

Leno keeping NBC up at night
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/LGN2/PJ/h]
By Meg James
'Tonight' host is said to be unhappy about his planned exit in 2009.

America's own unlawful combatants?
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/QCL4/PJ/h]
By Julian E. Barnes
Using private guards in Iraq could expose the U.S. to accusations of treaty
violations, some experts think.

Interstate 5 getting back in the flow
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/CNYG/PJ/h]
By Bettina Boxall and Mitchell Landsberg
Lanes are reopening after Friday's deadly tunnel crash, but it will be months
before the route returns to normal.

Taking the long view of architecture in Athens
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/CNY8/PJ/h]
By Tracy Wilkinson
Art Deco homes that interrupt the new Acropolis museum's vista of the Parthenon
are slated for demolition. Occupants say their buildings are an important part
of history too.

More Top Stories [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/BFD8/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



CALIFORNIA/LOCAL [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/RVTY/PJ/h]


Motorist fee increases signed by governor
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/BFDO/PJ/h]
By Patrick Mcgreevy
Registration costs will go up $3 per year for all vehicles and a smog abatement
charge on new cars will jump from $12 to $20 a year.

Flaws found in O.C. tollway foes' study
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/HLS7/PJ/h]
By Dan Weikel
The report had backed expanding the I-5 instead of building the turnpike. Toll
road officials call the analysis superficial, inaccurate.

Respecting, and liking, these elders
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/3YZT/PJ/h]
By Larry Gordon
USC gerontology students find that living with Kingsley Manor's senior citizens
is anything but old hat.

Suspected mob figure won't be returned to Italy
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/S2HS/PJ/h]
By Anna Gorman
A U.S. court, citing the risk of torture, denies Italy's request to extradite
Rosario Gambino.

More California/Local News
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/M83Y/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



NATIONAL [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/RVTJ/PJ/h]


Dying inmates may be given leniency
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/4I76/PJ/h]
By Richard B. Schmitt
New guidelines, which may be effective Nov. 1, would give judges greater power
over early releases.

Tiny wasp brings a big problem for crops in Hawaii
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/4IMO/PJ/h]
By Tomas Alex Tizon
An insect with African origins is killing off the wiliwili tree, which is a
windbreak of major agricultural importance.

U.N. envoy in Asia to press for Myanmar talks
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/ARD7/PJ/h]
By Paul Watson
Ibrahim Gambari will urge talks between the junta and opposition leader Suu Kyi.
He'll also meet with neighboring countries that oppose sanctions on the regime
after last month's violent crackdown.

More National News [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/ZWVG/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



WORLD [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/V76T/PJ/h]


In S. Korea, patriotism rears dragon-like head
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/2DZC/PJ/h]
By Bruce Wallace
Critics call the film 'D-War' a fantasy fiasco. But viewers see it as a national
treasure, and they're breathing fire on any naysayers.

Car bomb kills 10 near Baghdad shrine
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/V76B/PJ/h]
By Alexandra Zavis
Attack in an area frequently targeted by Sunni militants comes amid festivities
marking the end of the holy month of Ramadan.

No high hopes on Mideast trip, Rice says
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/D3EY/PJ/h]
By Ashraf Khalil
Her five-day visit is aimed at facilitating Israeli-Palestinian talks ahead of a
U.S. peace conference.

More World News [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/ARDK/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



BUSINESS [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/M8PP/PJ/h]


'Gangster' gets a shot
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/N6U1/PJ/h]
By Claudia Eller
The film is full of twists and turns -- and so is the saga of getting it to the
screen.

SEC's policy path wavers
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/79WK/PJ/h]
By Jonathan Peterson
The securities panel is losing its Democrats and Chairman Cox is giving mixed
signals.

Agents cast selves in writers' contract talks
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/4IMW/PJ/h]
By Richard Verrier and Lorenza Muñoz
As Hollywood braces for a possible strike next month, talent agents are trying
to do what they know best: Match-make.

'Why Did I Get Married?' takes No. 1 at the box office
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/WMO2/PJ/h]
By Josh Friedman
In a competitive weekend at the box office, "Tyler Perry's Why Did I Get
Married?" outsold films featuring stars George Clooney, Joaquin Phoenix and Cate
Blanchett and knocked "The Rock" out of first place.

More Business News [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/CNIJ/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



SPORTS [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/79WE/PJ/h]


BCS is through the looking glass
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/9JKS/PJ/h]
By Chris Dufresne
. . . Or is it down the rabbit hole? Whatever, the first Bowl Championship
Series standings are just plain weird, and they may keep getting weirder.

Patriots pop Cowboys' bubble
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/KBNB/PJ/h]
By Sam Farmer
New England emphatically wins clash of NFL unbeatens, then has some fun with
Owens' 'popcorn' reference afterward. Brady throws for five touchdowns.

Javelin thrower's case for steroids misses the mark
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/79WS/PJ/h]
By Helene Elliott
Kate Schmidt, twice an Olympic javelin bronze medalist and a former world-record
holder, made an earnest case in The Times' Opinion section Sunday for allowing
athletes to use performance-enhancing drugs that are now banned.

More Sports News [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/ARDN/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



ARTS AND ENTERTAINMENT
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/9JKN/PJ/h]


Fox Business Network opens for business
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/Y4CD/PJ/h]
By Matea Gold
The financial network is launching on Main Street America with the promise to
make the news interesting.

A writers' strike nobody wants
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/Y4CY/PJ/h]
By Scott Collins
HOW will we get to sleep without David Letterman's "Top 10 List"? Or Stephen
Colbert's "The Word"? What if we're left hanging with story interruptus on
"Heroes" or "Lost"? Is there life after " 'Til Death?"

Doris Lessing's Nobel: A victory for science fiction
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/5E5F/PJ/h]
By M.g. Lord
Her epic 'Canopus in Argos' series helped the genre break through to mainstream
literary respect.

More Arts and Entertainment News
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/M8PA/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



FEATURES [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/M8PZ/PJ/h]


Going for golden [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/1KAR/PJ/h]
By Shari Roan
The answer might be found in a few more birthdays.

In life, genes will only get you so far
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/6Q41/PJ/h]
By Susan Brink
Good health choices are still key to achieving longevity

More Features [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/8SXH/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



OPINION [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/D3E0/PJ/h]


Killer cow emissions [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/RVHI/PJ/h]
Livestock are a leading source of greenhouse gases. Why isn't anyone raising a
stink?

Labeling genocide won't halt it
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/6Q4B/PJ/h]
By Niall Ferguson
Armenians were murdered, but the current Turkish regime shouldn't be faulted for
what happened more than 90 years ago.

Clarence Thomas is not the hypocrite
[http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/9JKO/PJ/h]
By James Kirchick
It's not fair to use affirmative action against the Supreme Court's lone black
judge.

More Opinion [http://link.latimes.com/r/R58V3G/G0K/R3UBW7/THY/V76F/PJ/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

of today's front page
